title: Vernacular names record
description: Verified multilingual common names for one plant species. Machine-managed by scripts/verify_names.py — NOT hand-edited. Joined to plants/<id>.yaml by the shared `id`. Every name carries the authoritative source(s) that attest it; a name may never exist without at least one source (data-quality guarantee enforced structurally). Names/translations only — no medicinal content lives here.

scientific_name.description: Denormalized from the plant record so this file is readable / usable standalone.

names.description: Map of ISO 639-1 language code -> array of verified vernacular names for that language. Language codes must exist and be `enabled` in vocabularies/languages.yaml.

name.description: The vernacular name, in the source's original casing/orthography (NFC-normalized).

preferred.description: Exactly one name per language SHOULD be flagged preferred (the canonical display form). Chosen by: curated authority > most-attested > Wikipedia title.

sources.description: Provenance tokens: '<provider>:<id-or-dataset>'. Independence for the accept rule is counted across distinct tokens. A published (status: verified) name needs either >=1 curated-authority token (eppo:/powo:/col:/a curated gbif dataset) OR >=2 independent tokens.

status.description: verified = passed the strict multi-source accept rule and is served publicly. needs-review = single unconfirmed source, held back (NEVER served publicly).

romanization.description: Optional Latin-script transliteration for names in non-Latin scripts (Cyrillic/Greek). Aids search; never replaces the native form.

verification.description: Honest 'checked vs found' record, mirroring the plant schema's lookalikes_review pattern: a language in `checked_languages` but absent from `names` was searched and no verified name was found (a real gap), distinct from a language never checked.
